response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior engineer with strong full-stack credentials, solid systems-level exposure (DLang at Sociomantic with low-latency, high-throughput services), and genuine geospatial experience via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the core required skill — modern C++ (or Rust) — is largely absent from recent work. C/C++ experience is limited to a brief 2014-2015 stint at National Instruments, and there is no Rust experience listed. Recent years are dominated by TypeScript/Node.js. The geospatial background and distributed-systems exposure are relevant pluses, but the language/stack mismatch is significant for a role centered on C++ routing tile pipelines.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic (millions of req/sec)
Senior-level breadth, strong testing/TDD discipline, and cross-stack adaptability
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience; last C/C++ role was ~10 years ago at National Instruments
Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js rather than systems programming

key_factors.factor: Distributed low-latency systems exposure (DLang at Sociomantic)
